response.justification: The candidate has genuine geospatial domain passion (founded an open-source geo-data platform used by Green Map, spoke at FOSS4G) and real low-latency distributed-systems experience from Sociomantic, where he built asynchronous DLang services handling millions of requests per second on Linux. However, the role's primary requirement is modern C++ (or Rust) in commercial settings, and his C++ exposure is limited to a seven-month stint at National Instruments in 2014-2015 plus a university thesis; the last decade has been dominated by TypeScript/Node.js web and full-stack work. He also lacks direct experience with routing engines, tiling pipelines, or navigation guidance systems, so the fit hinges on whether DLang systems experience and GIS enthusiasm can transfer quickly.
strengths: Hands-on geospatial/GIS experience: founded and maintains an open-source geo-data mapping platform, FOSS4G speaker
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work in DLang (async I/O, fibers, caching) at millions of requests/second on Linux
Broad, adaptable engineer with strong testing/craftsmanship culture (TDD, CI/CD) and demonstrated willingness to learn new stacks
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dated (brief 2014-2015 role); no Rust experience listed
No production experience with routing data pipelines, map tiling, or navigation guidance systems
Recent roles (Naver, Tencent, RIDE) are TypeScript/Node web and product work, far from systems-level pipeline engineering

key_factors.factor: Lack of recent C++/Rust systems experience
